description: This pipeline is designed to process biomedical microscopic images, converting them into a suitable format for analysis and applying various operations such as segmentation, masking, and merging of channels. The pipeline takes input images, applies Cellpose segmentation to identify regions of interest, and then converts these regions into masks and overlays. The output includes the original image with added overlays of segmented areas, allowing for further analysis and visualization of the data.

e3caf8bc-e41c-4c36-9f19-9406740d66a7.name: ome-image-from-image-plus
e3caf8bc-e41c-4c36-9f19-9406740d66a7.description:
Converts an image into an OME image. Optionally allows the attachment of ROI that are then stored within the OME image

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

ba2239c5-d98a-4826-8728-ae34ee5526cd.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
ba2239c5-d98a-4826-8728-ae34ee5526cd.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

8db9199f-6882-45bf-9ba8-e368b687eb40.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
8db9199f-6882-45bf-9ba8-e368b687eb40.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

9c203491-1bf0-411e-a715-8f12d3b86509.name: ome-image-from-image-plus
9c203491-1bf0-411e-a715-8f12d3b86509.description:
Converts an image into an OME image. Optionally allows the attachment of ROI that are then stored within the OME image

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

082caa50-7cfb-4bfe-98d2-f7700559eb02.name: ij1-color-merge-channels
082caa50-7cfb-4bfe-98d2-f7700559eb02.description:
Merges each greyscale image plane into a multi-channel image. Implements the standard ImageJ algorithm for merging channels that either creates an RGB or a composite image. We recommend to use Blend images for creating renders and Merge channels (composite) that provide more options for the specific use cases.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

520fcda1-4b1f-44dc-835e-b798eac0e092.name: cellpose-2
520fcda1-4b1f-44dc-835e-b798eac0e092.description:
Deprecated. Use the node with the same name.

Runs Cellpose on the input image. This node supports both segmentation in 3D and executing Cellpose for each 2D image plane. This node can generate a multitude of outputs, although only ROI is activated by default. Go to the 'Outputs' parameter section to enable the other outputs.
  • Labels: A grayscale image where each connected component is assigned a unique value.
  • Flows XY: An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel
  • Flows Z: Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)
  • Flows d: Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)
  • Probabilities: An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel
  • ROI: ROI of the segmented areas.
Please note that you need to setup a valid Python environment with Cellpose installed. You can find the setting in Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose.

This algorithm merges all annotations and data annotations. Use the iteration step settings to determine how annotations and data annotations are merged

ffbb8b79-129f-4a9b-b67c-256dc9a89b97.name: ij1-roi-render-overlay
ffbb8b79-129f-4a9b-b67c-256dc9a89b97.description: Renders the overlay ROI of an image to RGB
